# routeros_routing_id (Resource)


## Example Usage
```terraform
resource "routeros_routing_id" "test" {
  name              = "router-id-test"
  router_id         = "10.10.10.10"
  select_dynamic_id = "any"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Reference name.

### Optional

- `comment` (String)
- `disabled` (Boolean) ID reference is not used.
- `router_id` (String) Parameter to explicitly set the Router ID. If not specified, it can be elected from one of the configured IP addresses on the router.
- `select_dynamic_id` (String) States what IP addresses to use for ID election.
- `select_from_vrf` (String) VRF from which to select IP addresses for the ID election.

### Read-Only

- `dynamic` (Boolean) Configuration item created by software, not by management interface. It is not exported, and cannot be directly modified.
- `dynamic_id` (String) Currently selected ID.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `inactive` (Boolean)

## Import
Import is supported using the following syntax:
```shell
#The ID can be found via API or the terminal
#The command for the terminal is -> :put [/routing/id get [print show-ids]]
terraform import routeros_routing_id.test "*1"
#Or you can import a resource using one of its attributes
terraform import routeros_routing_id.test "name=router-id-test"
```
